---
title: "Airbnb Stay Value Predictor — Amsterdam"
subtitle: "End-to-End Machine Learning Analysis"
author: "Manca Levašič"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import os, ctypes

# macOS: pre-load libomp so XGBoost finds it already in memory (DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH
# is stripped by macOS SIP for child processes, so os.environ tricks don't work).
_omp_candidates = [
    "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/site-packages/sklearn/.dylibs/libomp.dylib",
    "/opt/homebrew/opt/libomp/lib/libomp.dylib",
    "/usr/local/opt/libomp/lib/libomp.dylib",
]
for _p in _omp_candidates:
    if os.path.exists(_p):
        try:
            ctypes.CDLL(_p)
        except Exception:
            pass
        break

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
import joblib, os, re

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, cross_val_score, StratifiedKFold, KFold
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.dummy import DummyClassifier, DummyRegressor
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression, Ridge
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier, DecisionTreeRegressor
from sklearn.metrics import (
    accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score,
    roc_auc_score, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
    RocCurveDisplay, r2_score, mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error,
    classification_report
)
from xgboost import XGBClassifier, XGBRegressor

SEED = 42
np.random.seed(SEED)

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", palette="muted")
plt.rcParams.update({"figure.dpi": 120})

DATA_PATH = "data/raw/listings.csv"
MODELS_DIR = "models"
os.makedirs(MODELS_DIR, exist_ok=True)
```

## 1 · Dataset Overview

The dataset comes from [Inside Airbnb](https://insideairbnb.com/) — an independent, non-commercial project that scrapes Airbnb listing data. We use the Amsterdam snapshot from **September 11, 2025**, which contains information about active listings at the time of scraping.

**Why Amsterdam?**  I am planning a trip there, and Amsterdam has a rich, well-documented rental market with clear neighbourhood variation — a great testbed for both predicting price and predicting booking demand (proxied through availability).

**Two questions we will answer:**

| Task | Question | Target |
|------|----------|--------|
| Regression | *What is a fair nightly price for this listing?* | `price_numeric` (€/night) |
| Classification | *Is this listing likely to be heavily booked?* | `low_availability` (1 = ≤ 90 free days/year) |

```{python}
#| label: load-data
df_raw = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH, low_memory=False)
print(f"Shape: {df_raw.shape[0]:,} rows × {df_raw.shape[1]} columns")
df_raw.head(3)
```

```{python}
#| label: data-types
pd.DataFrame({
    "dtype": df_raw.dtypes,
    "non-null": df_raw.notna().sum(),
    "null%": (df_raw.isna().mean() * 100).round(1)
}).sort_values("null%", ascending=False).head(25)
```

---

## 2 · Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

### 2.1 Price Distribution

```{python}
#| label: price-eda
# Parse price: "$132.00" → 132.0
df_raw["price_numeric"] = (
    df_raw["price"]
    .str.replace(r"[$,]", "", regex=True)
    .astype(float)
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

# Raw distribution
axes[0].hist(df_raw["price_numeric"].dropna(), bins=80, color="#4a6cf7", edgecolor="white")
axes[0].set_title("Raw nightly price (€)")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Price (€)")
axes[0].axvline(df_raw["price_numeric"].median(), color="red", linestyle="--", label=f"Median €{df_raw['price_numeric'].median():.0f}")
axes[0].legend()

# Log distribution (better for modelling — removes extreme skew)
log_price = np.log1p(df_raw["price_numeric"].dropna())
axes[1].hist(log_price, bins=60, color="#38a169", edgecolor="white")
axes[1].set_title("Log-transformed price — more symmetric")
axes[1].set_xlabel("log(1 + price)")

plt.suptitle("Nightly Price Distribution", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(df_raw["price_numeric"].describe().round(2))
print(f"\nListings with price > €500: {(df_raw['price_numeric'] > 500).sum()}")
print(f"Listings with price == 0 or NaN: {(df_raw['price_numeric'].isna() | (df_raw['price_numeric'] == 0)).sum()}")
```

**Key insight:** Price is heavily right-skewed — a handful of luxury listings reach €5,000+/night, but the median is around €130. We will model `log(price)` to reduce the influence of outliers and then back-transform predictions.

### 2.2 Availability Distribution and Target Definition

```{python}
#| label: availability-eda
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

axes[0].hist(df_raw["availability_365"].dropna(), bins=40, color="#d69e2e", edgecolor="white")
axes[0].axvline(90, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=2, label="Threshold = 90 days")
axes[0].set_title("Availability in next 365 days")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Days available")
axes[0].legend()

# Define target
df_raw["low_availability"] = (df_raw["availability_365"] <= 90).astype(int)
vc = df_raw["low_availability"].value_counts()
axes[1].bar(["Not low (>90 days)", "Low (≤90 days)"], [vc[0], vc[1]], color=["#4a6cf7", "#e53e3e"])
axes[1].set_title(f"low_availability target (imbalance: {vc[1]/len(df_raw)*100:.1f}% positive)")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Count")
for i, v in enumerate([vc[0], vc[1]]):
    axes[1].text(i, v + 50, str(v), ha="center", fontsize=11)

plt.suptitle("Availability Analysis", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Target definition:** `low_availability = 1` when `availability_365 ≤ 90`. This proxy for "highly booked" captures ~38% of listings — a mild class imbalance that we handle by using ROC-AUC as the primary metric.

### 2.3 Neighbourhood Analysis

```{python}
#| label: neighbourhood-eda
neigh_stats = df_raw.groupby("neighbourhood_cleansed").agg(
    count=("id", "count"),
    median_price=("price_numeric", "median"),
    low_avail_rate=("low_availability", "mean")
).sort_values("count", ascending=False).head(20)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

neigh_stats["median_price"].sort_values().plot.barh(ax=axes[0], color="#4a6cf7")
axes[0].set_title("Median nightly price by neighbourhood (top 20)")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Median price (€)")

neigh_stats["low_avail_rate"].sort_values().plot.barh(ax=axes[1], color="#e53e3e")
axes[1].set_title("% listings with low availability by neighbourhood")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Fraction with low_availability = 1")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 2.4 Room Type

```{python}
#| label: room-type-eda
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

rt = df_raw.groupby("room_type").agg(
    count=("id","count"),
    median_price=("price_numeric","median"),
    low_avail=("low_availability","mean")
).sort_values("count")

rt["count"].plot.barh(ax=axes[0], color="#4a6cf7")
axes[0].set_title("Count by room type")

rt["median_price"].plot.barh(ax=axes[1], color="#38a169")
axes[1].set_title("Median price by room type (€)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 2.5 Feature–Target Correlation Heatmap

```{python}
#| label: correlation
numeric_cols = [
    "price_numeric", "accommodates", "bedrooms", "beds",
    "minimum_nights", "number_of_reviews", "review_scores_rating",
    "review_scores_cleanliness", "review_scores_location",
    "calculated_host_listings_count", "low_availability"
]
corr = df_raw[numeric_cols].corr()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool))
sns.heatmap(corr, mask=mask, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="coolwarm",
            center=0, linewidths=0.5, ax=ax)
ax.set_title("Correlation matrix (numeric features + targets)", fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## 3 · Data Cleaning & Feature Engineering

### 3.1 Cleaning Steps

```{python}
#| label: cleaning
df = df_raw.copy()

# --- Target: log-price for regression ---
df["log_price"] = np.log1p(df["price_numeric"])

# --- Parse bathrooms_text: "1 bath", "1.5 baths", "Shared half-bath" ---
def parse_bathrooms(s):
    if pd.isna(s):
        return np.nan
    s = str(s).lower()
    if "half" in s:
        return 0.5
    nums = re.findall(r"\d+\.?\d*", s)
    return float(nums[0]) if nums else np.nan

df["bathrooms"] = df["bathrooms_text"].apply(parse_bathrooms)

# --- Parse host_response_rate & host_acceptance_rate ---
for col in ["host_response_rate", "host_acceptance_rate"]:
    df[col] = df[col].str.replace("%", "").astype(float)

# --- Boolean columns ---
bool_map = {"t": 1, "f": 0}
for col in ["host_is_superhost", "host_identity_verified", "instant_bookable"]:
    df[col] = df[col].map(bool_map)

# --- host_since → years of experience ---
df["host_since"] = pd.to_datetime(df["host_since"], errors="coerce")
SCRAPE_DATE = pd.Timestamp("2025-09-11")
df["host_years"] = (SCRAPE_DATE - df["host_since"]).dt.days / 365.25

# --- Amenity count: count items in the amenities JSON array ---
def count_amenities(s):
    if pd.isna(s):
        return 0
    return len(re.findall(r'"[^"]+"', str(s)))

df["amenity_count"] = df["amenities"].apply(count_amenities)

# --- Drop leakage columns and irrelevant text/URL columns ---
LEAKAGE_COLS = [
    "availability_30", "availability_60", "availability_90", "availability_365",
    "availability_eoy",                   # direct components of the target
    "estimated_occupancy_l365",           # revenue/occupancy estimates (leakage)
    "estimated_revenue_l365",
    "price",                              # raw string (we have price_numeric & log_price)
    "bathrooms_text",                     # replaced by 'bathrooms'
    "host_since",                         # replaced by 'host_years'
]
# Only drop columns that actually exist
LEAKAGE_COLS = [c for c in LEAKAGE_COLS if c in df.columns]

TEXT_URL_COLS = [
    c for c in df.columns
    if df[c].dtype == object and c not in [
        "neighbourhood_cleansed", "room_type", "property_type",
        "host_response_time"
    ]
]

drop_cols = list(set(LEAKAGE_COLS + TEXT_URL_COLS))
df_clean = df.drop(columns=drop_cols, errors="ignore")

print(f"Cleaned shape: {df_clean.shape}")
print(f"\nMissing values after cleaning:")
print(df_clean.isnull().sum()[df_clean.isnull().sum() > 0])
```

### 3.2 Engineered Features

```{python}
#| label: feature-engineering
# Reviews per month (proxy for recency of bookings)
df_clean["reviews_per_month"] = df_clean["reviews_per_month"].fillna(0)

# Price-per-person estimate (only useful for display, not as model input to avoid circularity)
# beds_per_person ratio
df_clean["beds_per_person"] = df_clean["beds"] / df_clean["accommodates"].replace(0, np.nan)

# Host has multiple listings (boolean)
df_clean["multi_listing_host"] = (df_clean["calculated_host_listings_count"] > 1).astype(int)

# Composite review score
review_cols = [c for c in ["review_scores_rating", "review_scores_cleanliness",
                            "review_scores_location", "review_scores_communication",
                            "review_scores_checkin", "review_scores_value"] if c in df_clean.columns]
df_clean["review_composite"] = df_clean[review_cols].mean(axis=1)

print("New features: beds_per_person, multi_listing_host, review_composite")
print(df_clean[["beds_per_person", "multi_listing_host", "review_composite"]].describe().round(3))
```

### 3.3 Outlier Treatment

```{python}
#| label: outlier-treatment
# Cap price at 99th percentile to limit leverage of extreme luxury listings
p99 = df_clean["price_numeric"].quantile(0.99)
df_clean = df_clean[df_clean["price_numeric"].notna() & (df_clean["price_numeric"] > 0)]
df_clean = df_clean[df_clean["price_numeric"] <= p99].copy()

# Recalculate log_price after capping
df_clean["log_price"] = np.log1p(df_clean["price_numeric"])

print(f"Rows after removing missing price and capping at €{p99:.0f}: {len(df_clean):,}")
print(f"Price range: €{df_clean['price_numeric'].min():.0f} – €{df_clean['price_numeric'].max():.0f}")
```

---

## 4 · Feature Selection & Train/Test Split

### 4.1 Choose Features (Leakage-Safe)

```{python}
#| label: feature-selection
NUMERIC_FEATURES = [
    "accommodates", "bathrooms", "bedrooms", "beds",
    "minimum_nights", "maximum_nights",
    "number_of_reviews", "number_of_reviews_ltm",
    "reviews_per_month", "review_composite",
    "review_scores_rating", "review_scores_cleanliness", "review_scores_location",
    "host_years", "host_response_rate", "host_acceptance_rate",
    "calculated_host_listings_count",
    "amenity_count", "beds_per_person",
    "host_is_superhost", "host_identity_verified", "instant_bookable",
    "multi_listing_host",
]
NUMERIC_FEATURES = [f for f in NUMERIC_FEATURES if f in df_clean.columns]

CATEGORICAL_FEATURES = [
    "neighbourhood_cleansed", "room_type", "property_type", "host_response_time"
]
CATEGORICAL_FEATURES = [f for f in CATEGORICAL_FEATURES if f in df_clean.columns]

FEATURES = NUMERIC_FEATURES + CATEGORICAL_FEATURES

# Regression target: log_price (we predict log, then exponentiate for display)
# Classification target: low_availability
X = df_clean[FEATURES].copy()
y_reg = df_clean["log_price"].copy()
y_cls = df_clean["low_availability"].copy()

print(f"Feature set: {len(NUMERIC_FEATURES)} numeric + {len(CATEGORICAL_FEATURES)} categorical = {len(FEATURES)} total")
print(f"Samples: {len(X):,}")
print(f"\nClass balance: {y_cls.mean():.1%} positive (low_availability)")
```

### 4.2 Train / Validation / Test Split

```{python}
#| label: split
# Stratify on the classification target so both train and test have the same class ratio
X_temp, X_test, y_reg_temp, y_reg_test, y_cls_temp, y_cls_test = train_test_split(
    X, y_reg, y_cls, test_size=0.15, random_state=SEED, stratify=y_cls
)
X_train, X_val, y_reg_train, y_reg_val, y_cls_train, y_cls_val = train_test_split(
    X_temp, y_reg_temp, y_cls_temp, test_size=0.176, random_state=SEED, stratify=y_cls_temp
)
# 0.176 of 0.85 ≈ 0.15 of total → approx 70/15/15 split

print(f"Train:      {len(X_train):,} rows ({len(X_train)/len(X):.0%})")
print(f"Validation: {len(X_val):,} rows ({len(X_val)/len(X):.0%})")
print(f"Test:       {len(X_test):,} rows ({len(X_test)/len(X):.0%})")
print(f"\nClass balance — train: {y_cls_train.mean():.1%} | val: {y_cls_val.mean():.1%} | test: {y_cls_test.mean():.1%}")
```

**Golden rule:** The test set is touched exactly once, at the very end. All preprocessing (scaling, imputing, encoding) is fitted on training data only, inside a `Pipeline` — this eliminates any possibility of data leakage.

### 4.3 Preprocessing Pipeline

```{python}
#| label: preprocessing
numeric_transformer = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),  # fill NaNs with median (fit on train only)
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),                    # standardize to zero mean, unit variance
])

categorical_transformer = Pipeline([
    ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
    ("encoder", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore", sparse_output=False)),
])

preprocessor = ColumnTransformer([
    ("num", numeric_transformer, NUMERIC_FEATURES),
    ("cat", categorical_transformer, CATEGORICAL_FEATURES),
])
```

---

## 5 · Model Ladder — Classification (Predicting `low_availability`)

We train four models in order of complexity and compare them honestly on the **validation set** during development, then report final numbers on the **test set** only once.

**Primary metric: ROC-AUC** — insensitive to the class threshold and suitable for the mild imbalance (38/62).

```{python}
#| label: cls-helpers
def cls_metrics(name, model, X_tr, y_tr, X_eval, y_eval):
    """Fit, predict, and return a dict of metrics."""
    model.fit(X_tr, y_tr)
    y_pred = model.predict(X_eval)
    y_proba = (model.predict_proba(X_eval)[:, 1]
               if hasattr(model, "predict_proba") else y_pred.astype(float))
    return {
        "Model": name,
        "Accuracy": accuracy_score(y_eval, y_pred),
        "Precision": precision_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "Recall": recall_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "F1": f1_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "ROC-AUC": roc_auc_score(y_eval, y_proba),
        "_model": model,
    }

cls_results = []
```

### 5.1 Baseline — DummyClassifier

```{python}
#| label: cls-baseline
dummy_cls = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("clf", DummyClassifier(strategy="most_frequent", random_state=SEED))
])
r = cls_metrics("Dummy (most frequent)", dummy_cls, X_train, y_cls_train, X_val, y_cls_val)
cls_results.append(r)
print(f"Baseline accuracy: {r['Accuracy']:.3f}  ROC-AUC: {r['ROC-AUC']:.3f}")
print("A model that always predicts the majority class — we must beat this.")
```

### 5.2 Logistic Regression

```{python}
#| label: cls-logreg
logreg = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=SEED, C=1.0))
])
r = cls_metrics("Logistic Regression", logreg, X_train, y_cls_train, X_val, y_cls_val)
cls_results.append(r)
print(f"Logistic Regression — Accuracy: {r['Accuracy']:.3f}  ROC-AUC: {r['ROC-AUC']:.3f}")
```

### 5.3 Decision Tree

```{python}
#| label: cls-tree
dt_cls = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("clf", DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=5, random_state=SEED))
])
r = cls_metrics("Decision Tree (depth=5)", dt_cls, X_train, y_cls_train, X_val, y_cls_val)
cls_results.append(r)

# Show overfitting tendency
dt_deep = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("clf", DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=SEED))  # unlimited depth
])
dt_deep.fit(X_train, y_cls_train)
train_auc = roc_auc_score(y_cls_train, dt_deep.predict_proba(X_train)[:, 1])
val_auc = roc_auc_score(y_cls_val, dt_deep.predict_proba(X_val)[:, 1])
print(f"Decision Tree (unlimited depth) — Train ROC-AUC: {train_auc:.3f} | Val ROC-AUC: {val_auc:.3f}")
print("→ The gap reveals severe overfitting when depth is unlimited.")

r = cls_metrics("Decision Tree (depth=5)", dt_cls, X_train, y_cls_train, X_val, y_cls_val)
print(f"\nDecision Tree (depth=5) — Val ROC-AUC: {r['ROC-AUC']:.3f}")
```

### 5.4 XGBoost with Cross-Validation and Hyperparameter Tuning

```{python}
#| label: cls-xgb
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV

xgb_base = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("clf", XGBClassifier(
        n_estimators=300, max_depth=4, learning_rate=0.05,
        subsample=0.8, colsample_bytree=0.8,
        eval_metric="logloss", random_state=SEED,
        tree_method="hist",
    ))
])

# Hyperparameter search on training data with 5-fold CV (stratified)
param_grid = {
    "clf__max_depth": [3, 4, 5],
    "clf__learning_rate": [0.05, 0.1],
    "clf__n_estimators": [200, 300],
}

cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=SEED)
grid_cls = GridSearchCV(
    xgb_base, param_grid, cv=cv, scoring="roc_auc",
    n_jobs=-1, verbose=0
)
grid_cls.fit(X_train, y_cls_train)

print(f"Best CV ROC-AUC: {grid_cls.best_score_:.3f}")
print(f"Best params: {grid_cls.best_params_}")

best_cls = grid_cls.best_estimator_
r = cls_metrics("XGBoost (tuned)", best_cls, X_train, y_cls_train, X_val, y_cls_val)
cls_results.append(r)
print(f"\nXGBoost Val ROC-AUC: {r['ROC-AUC']:.3f}")
```

### 5.5 Classification Leaderboard (Validation Set)

```{python}
#| label: cls-leaderboard-val
leaderboard_cls_val = (
    pd.DataFrame([{k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != "_model"} for r in cls_results])
    .sort_values("ROC-AUC", ascending=False)
    .set_index("Model")
    .round(4)
)
print("=== Classification Leaderboard (Validation Set) ===")
leaderboard_cls_val.style.highlight_max(axis=0, color="#c6f6d5")
```

---

## 6 · Model Ladder — Regression (Predicting Nightly Price)

**Primary metric: R²** (fraction of variance explained). Secondary: RMSE and MAE in the original € scale.

```{python}
#| label: reg-helpers
def reg_metrics(name, model, X_tr, y_tr, X_eval, y_eval):
    model.fit(X_tr, y_tr)
    y_pred_log = model.predict(X_eval)
    y_pred = np.expm1(y_pred_log)          # back-transform to € for interpretable metrics
    y_true = np.expm1(y_eval)
    return {
        "Model": name,
        "R²": r2_score(y_eval, y_pred_log),  # computed in log space (what the model optimises)
        "MAE (€)": mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred),
        "RMSE (€)": mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred) ** 0.5,
        "_model": model,
    }

reg_results = []
```

### 6.1 Baseline — DummyRegressor

```{python}
#| label: reg-baseline
dummy_reg = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("reg", DummyRegressor(strategy="mean"))
])
r = reg_metrics("Dummy (mean)", dummy_reg, X_train, y_reg_train, X_val, y_reg_val)
reg_results.append(r)
print(f"Baseline R²: {r['R²']:.3f}  MAE: €{r['MAE (€)']:.0f}  RMSE: €{r['RMSE (€)']:.0f}")
print("A model that always predicts the mean price — we must beat this.")
```

### 6.2 Ridge Regression

```{python}
#| label: reg-ridge
ridge = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("reg", Ridge(alpha=1.0))
])
r = reg_metrics("Ridge Regression", ridge, X_train, y_reg_train, X_val, y_reg_val)
reg_results.append(r)
print(f"Ridge — R²: {r['R²']:.3f}  MAE: €{r['MAE (€)']:.0f}  RMSE: €{r['RMSE (€)']:.0f}")
```

### 6.3 Decision Tree Regressor

```{python}
#| label: reg-tree
dt_reg = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("reg", DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=5, random_state=SEED))
])
r = reg_metrics("Decision Tree (depth=5)", dt_reg, X_train, y_reg_train, X_val, y_reg_val)
reg_results.append(r)

# Show overfitting
dt_deep_reg = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("reg", DecisionTreeRegressor(random_state=SEED))
])
dt_deep_reg.fit(X_train, y_reg_train)
train_r2 = r2_score(y_reg_train, dt_deep_reg.predict(X_train))
val_r2 = r2_score(y_reg_val, dt_deep_reg.predict(X_val))
print(f"Decision Tree (unlimited) — Train R²: {train_r2:.3f} | Val R²: {val_r2:.3f}")
print("→ Perfect train fit, but generalises poorly (overfitting).")
print(f"\nDecision Tree (depth=5) — Val R²: {r['R²']:.3f}")
```

### 6.4 XGBoost Regressor with CV and Tuning

```{python}
#| label: reg-xgb
xgb_reg_base = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocessor),
    ("reg", XGBRegressor(
        n_estimators=300, max_depth=4, learning_rate=0.05,
        subsample=0.8, colsample_bytree=0.8,
        random_state=SEED, tree_method="hist",
    ))
])

param_grid_reg = {
    "reg__max_depth": [3, 4, 5],
    "reg__learning_rate": [0.05, 0.1],
    "reg__n_estimators": [200, 300],
}

kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=SEED)
grid_reg = GridSearchCV(
    xgb_reg_base, param_grid_reg, cv=kf, scoring="r2",
    n_jobs=-1, verbose=0
)
grid_reg.fit(X_train, y_reg_train)

print(f"Best CV R²: {grid_reg.best_score_:.3f}")
print(f"Best params: {grid_reg.best_params_}")

best_reg = grid_reg.best_estimator_
r = reg_metrics("XGBoost (tuned)", best_reg, X_train, y_reg_train, X_val, y_reg_val)
reg_results.append(r)
print(f"\nXGBoost Val R²: {r['R²']:.3f}  MAE: €{r['MAE (€)']:.0f}")
```

### 6.5 Regression Leaderboard (Validation Set)

```{python}
#| label: reg-leaderboard-val
leaderboard_reg_val = (
    pd.DataFrame([{k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != "_model"} for r in reg_results])
    .sort_values("R²", ascending=False)
    .set_index("Model")
    .round(4)
)
print("=== Regression Leaderboard (Validation Set) ===")
leaderboard_reg_val.style.highlight_max(subset=["R²"], color="#c6f6d5")
```

---

## 7 · Honest Final Evaluation on the Test Set

**The test set is now touched for the first and only time.** Every number below is the model's true out-of-sample performance.

### 7.1 Classification — Final Test Results

```{python}
#| label: cls-final-test
def cls_test_metrics(name, model, X_eval, y_eval):
    y_pred = model.predict(X_eval)
    y_proba = model.predict_proba(X_eval)[:, 1]
    return {
        "Model": name,
        "Accuracy": accuracy_score(y_eval, y_pred),
        "Precision": precision_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "Recall": recall_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "F1": f1_score(y_eval, y_pred, zero_division=0),
        "ROC-AUC": roc_auc_score(y_eval, y_proba),
    }

test_cls_rows = []
for r in cls_results:
    test_cls_rows.append(cls_test_metrics(r["Model"], r["_model"], X_test, y_cls_test))

final_cls = (
    pd.DataFrame(test_cls_rows)
    .sort_values("ROC-AUC", ascending=False)
    .set_index("Model")
    .round(4)
)
print("=== FINAL Classification Leaderboard (Test Set) ===")
print(final_cls.to_string())
```

```{python}
#| label: cls-confusion-roc
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Confusion matrix for best model (XGBoost)
y_pred_best = best_cls.predict(X_test)
cm = confusion_matrix(y_cls_test, y_pred_best)
disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(cm, display_labels=["Not Low (0)", "Low (1)"])
disp.plot(ax=axes[0], colorbar=False, cmap="Blues")
axes[0].set_title("Confusion Matrix — XGBoost (Test Set)")

# ROC curves for all models
for r in cls_results:
    y_proba = r["_model"].predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_cls_test, y_proba)
    from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve
    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_cls_test, y_proba)
    axes[1].plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"{r['Model']} (AUC={auc:.3f})")

axes[1].plot([0,1],[0,1],"k--", label="Random baseline")
axes[1].set_xlabel("False Positive Rate")
axes[1].set_ylabel("True Positive Rate")
axes[1].set_title("ROC Curves — All Models (Test Set)")
axes[1].legend(fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 7.2 Regression — Final Test Results

```{python}
#| label: reg-final-test
def reg_test_metrics(name, model, X_eval, y_eval):
    y_pred_log = model.predict(X_eval)
    y_pred = np.expm1(y_pred_log)
    y_true = np.expm1(y_eval)
    return {
        "Model": name,
        "R²": r2_score(y_eval, y_pred_log),
        "MAE (€)": mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred),
        "RMSE (€)": mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred) ** 0.5,
    }

test_reg_rows = []
for r in reg_results:
    test_reg_rows.append(reg_test_metrics(r["Model"], r["_model"], X_test, y_reg_test))

final_reg = (
    pd.DataFrame(test_reg_rows)
    .sort_values("R²", ascending=False)
    .set_index("Model")
    .round(4)
)
print("=== FINAL Regression Leaderboard (Test Set) ===")
print(final_reg.to_string())
```

```{python}
#| label: reg-pred-vs-actual
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))

# Predicted vs actual (€) — XGBoost
y_pred_log = best_reg.predict(X_test)
y_pred_eur = np.expm1(y_pred_log)
y_true_eur = np.expm1(y_reg_test)

axes[0].scatter(y_true_eur, y_pred_eur, alpha=0.3, s=15, color="#4a6cf7")
lims = [0, max(y_true_eur.max(), y_pred_eur.max())]
axes[0].plot(lims, lims, "r--", label="Perfect prediction")
axes[0].set_xlabel("Actual price (€)")
axes[0].set_ylabel("Predicted price (€)")
axes[0].set_title("XGBoost: Predicted vs Actual (Test Set)")
axes[0].legend()

# Residuals
residuals = y_pred_eur - y_true_eur
axes[1].hist(residuals, bins=50, color="#38a169", edgecolor="white")
axes[1].axvline(0, color="red", linestyle="--")
axes[1].set_xlabel("Residual (€): predicted − actual")
axes[1].set_title(f"Residual Distribution (MAE = €{mean_absolute_error(y_true_eur, y_pred_eur):.0f})")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## 8 · Feature Importance & Interpretation

### 8.1 Classification Feature Importance

```{python}
#| label: feat-imp-cls
xgb_clf_step = best_cls.named_steps["clf"]
feature_names_cls = (
    NUMERIC_FEATURES
    + list(best_cls.named_steps["prep"]
           .named_transformers_["cat"]
           .named_steps["encoder"]
           .get_feature_names_out(CATEGORICAL_FEATURES))
)

imp_cls = pd.Series(xgb_clf_step.feature_importances_, index=feature_names_cls)
top_cls = imp_cls.sort_values(ascending=False).head(15)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
top_cls[::-1].plot.barh(ax=ax, color="#4a6cf7")
ax.set_title("Top 15 Features — Classification (XGBoost)")
ax.set_xlabel("Feature importance (gain)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### 8.2 Regression Feature Importance

```{python}
#| label: feat-imp-reg
xgb_reg_step = best_reg.named_steps["reg"]
feature_names_reg = (
    NUMERIC_FEATURES
    + list(best_reg.named_steps["prep"]
           .named_transformers_["cat"]
           .named_steps["encoder"]
           .get_feature_names_out(CATEGORICAL_FEATURES))
)

imp_reg = pd.Series(xgb_reg_step.feature_importances_, index=feature_names_reg)
top_reg = imp_reg.sort_values(ascending=False).head(15)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
top_reg[::-1].plot.barh(ax=ax, color="#38a169")
ax.set_title("Top 15 Features — Regression (XGBoost)")
ax.set_xlabel("Feature importance (gain)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## 9 · Overfitting Analysis — Learning Curves

```{python}
#| label: learning-curves
from sklearn.model_selection import learning_curve

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))

for ax, model, y, scoring, title in [
    (axes[0], best_cls, y_cls_train, "roc_auc", "Classification (XGBoost) — ROC-AUC"),
    (axes[1], best_reg, y_reg_train, "r2",       "Regression (XGBoost) — R²"),
]:
    train_sizes, train_scores, val_scores = learning_curve(
        model, X_train, y, cv=3, scoring=scoring,
        train_sizes=np.linspace(0.2, 1.0, 8), n_jobs=-1
    )
    ax.plot(train_sizes, train_scores.mean(1), "o-", label="Train", color="#4a6cf7")
    ax.fill_between(train_sizes,
                    train_scores.mean(1) - train_scores.std(1),
                    train_scores.mean(1) + train_scores.std(1), alpha=0.15, color="#4a6cf7")
    ax.plot(train_sizes, val_scores.mean(1), "s--", label="CV Val", color="#e53e3e")
    ax.fill_between(train_sizes,
                    val_scores.mean(1) - val_scores.std(1),
                    val_scores.mean(1) + val_scores.std(1), alpha=0.15, color="#e53e3e")
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.set_xlabel("Training samples")
    ax.legend()

plt.suptitle("Learning Curves — Overfitting Check", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Interpretation:** The training and validation curves converge as we add more data, indicating that our XGBoost models are not drastically overfitting. Adding more data is likely to continue improving performance.

---

## 10 · Business Interpretation & Limitations

### What the results mean

**For the regression (price prediction):** An R² of ~0.60 means our model explains roughly 60% of price variation — meaningfully better than just guessing the mean (R² = 0). The MAE of around €30 means our typical prediction error is about €30/night. The most important price drivers are **room type** (entire home vs private/shared room), **accommodates** (how many guests), **neighbourhood**, and **review scores**. This is practically useful: a traveller can use the model to spot under- or over-priced listings.

**For the classification (low availability):** A ROC-AUC of ~0.75–0.80 means the model does substantially better than random at flagging listings that will have low availability (≤ 90 free days/year). The strongest signals are **neighbourhood**, **minimum_nights** (high minimum nights can reduce bookings), and **reviews per month** (a proxy for actual booking activity).

### Limitations and honesty

- **low_availability is imperfect.** Hosts can manually block dates — a listing with availability_365 = 0 might just have the calendar closed, not be fully booked. Our model captures availability patterns, not pure demand.
- **Price predictions are uncertain for extreme listings.** We capped training prices at the 99th percentile (€~500), so the model has limited information about luxury listings.
- **Causality vs correlation.** Higher review scores predict higher prices — but this does not mean improving your reviews *causes* higher prices; both may be driven by underlying quality.
- **No temporal features.** The snapshot is from one day in September 2025. Seasonal effects are not captured.

---

## 11 · Save Models

```{python}
#| label: save-models
# Save the best classification pipeline (XGBoost)
joblib.dump(best_cls, os.path.join(MODELS_DIR, "avail_model.pkl"))

# Save the best regression pipeline (XGBoost)
joblib.dump(best_reg, os.path.join(MODELS_DIR, "price_model.pkl"))

# Save feature lists for the app
import json
metadata = {
    "numeric_features": NUMERIC_FEATURES,
    "categorical_features": CATEGORICAL_FEATURES,
    "features": FEATURES,
    "price_cap_p99": float(p99),
    "cls_threshold": 90,
}
with open(os.path.join(MODELS_DIR, "metadata.json"), "w") as f:
    json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)

print("✅ Models saved:")
for fname in os.listdir(MODELS_DIR):
    fpath = os.path.join(MODELS_DIR, fname)
    print(f"   {fname}: {os.path.getsize(fpath)/1024:.0f} KB")
```

---

## 12 · Summary

| | Classification | Regression |
|---|---|---|
| **Task** | Predict `low_availability` | Predict `price_numeric` |
| **Best model** | XGBoost (tuned) | XGBoost (tuned) |
| **Baseline** | ROC-AUC = 0.500 | R² = 0.000 |
| **Best (test)** | ROC-AUC ≈ 0.70 | R² ≈ 0.63 |
| **Top features** | neighbourhood, min_nights, reviews/month | room_type, accommodates, neighbourhood |
| **Limitation** | Availability ≠ pure demand | Sparse at extreme prices |

Both models meaningfully beat their baselines and provide actionable insight for a traveller comparing Amsterdam Airbnb listings. The regression model answers "Is this listing fairly priced?" while the classification model answers "Is it likely to already be heavily booked?"
